top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 102, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Tour de Tucson's mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Tour de Tucson's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before November.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 102 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Tour de Tucson. Train it until it feels boring.
At Tour de Tucson, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Tour de Tucson's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 102 miles.
102 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 3,500ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Tour de Tucson, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.heat: November in Tucson, Arizona, USA can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Tour de Tucson. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Tour de Tucson’s 102 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Tour de Tucson. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, VO2max, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 102 miles of mixed terrain in Tucson, Arizona, USA.
